codeReview.summary: The fix correctly addresses all three items from the feature description and resolves the blocking auth issue from the prior review. (1) authenticateRequest() is properly imported from _shared/auth.ts and called at the start of the POST handler, before any business logic or Groq API calls. AuthError is caught in the main catch block and returns a 401 response, following an acceptable pattern consistent with the codebase. (2) All 6 green hex color instances (#2EA866, #9FE8B3, #141c17, #132218, plus rgba(46,168,102,...)) in WorkflowGeneratorTab.tsx have been replaced with orange/amber equivalents (#f97316, #fdba74, #1c1510, #1a1408, rgba(249,115,22,...)). (3) The StudioRightPanel test timeout was increased from the default 5s to 15s, resolving the flaky timeout. Verification shows typecheck, tests, and lint all pass with no regressions (9 failed files, all pre-existing; 49 passed files, 407 tests pass).

issues.description: Console.log still outputs raw user prompt: 'Generating workflow for prompt: ${prompt}'. This was flagged as non-blocking in the prior review. While server-side, consider redacting or truncating user input in production logs for privacy hygiene. Not in scope for this fix feature.

The test file was committed as a new file (166 additions) rather than a modification. This is because git root is at /Users/gratitud3 rather than the project directory, so the file was previously untracked. The content is correct and the 15_000ms timeout is properly applied to the specific test case that was timing out.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The auth error handling pattern in generate-workflow differs slightly from groq-chat. groq-chat wraps authenticateRequest in its own try/catch within the handler, while generate-workflow checks instanceof AuthError in the main catch block. Both are valid but the codebase lacks a single canonical pattern. AGENTS.md documents 'Auth: await authenticateRequest(req.headers)' but doesn't specify the error handling pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: supabase/functions/generate-workflow/index.ts:322-324 uses instanceof AuthError in main catch. supabase/functions/groq-chat/index.ts:17-21 uses dedicated try/catch around authenticateRequest.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the fullstack-worker skill procedure correctly: read feature description, checked AGENTS.md and library, planned implementation, implemented backend first (auth) then frontend (colors), ran all validators. Worker skipped step 7 (agent-browser interactive verification) which was reasonable given the nature of the changes (auth guard, color values, test timeout) — all verifiable through grep and test output.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows systematic approach: read mission files → read auth pattern from groq-chat → implement auth → implement colors → fix test → run tsc/test/lint. handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The git root is at the home directory (/Users/gratitud3) rather than the project directory, causing the StudioRightPanel.test.tsx to appear as a new file in the commit even though it existed pre-commit. This is an environment configuration issue that could confuse future reviews.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git rev-parse --show-toplevel returns /Users/gratitud3. Commit 0ea18a7 shows StudioRightPanel.test.tsx as 'new file mode 100644' with 166 additions.

summary: PASS. The fix adequately addresses the blocking failure from the prior review (missing authenticateRequest in generate-workflow) and both additional items in the feature description (green→orange color migration in WorkflowGeneratorTab.tsx, StudioRightPanel test timeout fix). Auth is properly placed before any business logic with correct 401 error handling. All 6 green hex colors are replaced with orange/amber equivalents. Test timeout increased to 15s. Validators confirm no regressions: typecheck clean, 49 test files pass (407 tests), 9 pre-existing failures unchanged.
